what_it_does: This file is a generated ledger that records UI element candidates extracted from design mockup HTML files, the rule set used to select them, and per-element metadata linking each candidate to an ancestor region address, a human-readable dossier of capabilities/representation/code, and any self-registered ui:// address.
observations: The ledger reports 940 candidates generated from 23 mockups.

A top-level filter object documents the selection heuristics: always keep elements carrying data-ui-ref, privilege semantic tags, apply a special div/span rule based on own direct text length, and skip decorative or fragment classes and control-ancestor contexts.
Each candidate carries mockup_file, DOM selector, outerHTML, visible_text, tag, ancestor_address, base_address, self_registered flag, and self_address.
Every candidate is annotated with an ancestor_dossier containing region, capabilities, represents, code path, and a howto narrative.
Self-registered elements expose stable ui:// addresses such as ui://toolbar/run and ui://canvas.
The file deliberately avoids deduplication, noting that RG5 REDUCE will collapse recurring surfaces later.
Examples shown come from A1-canvas-empty-desktop.html and A11-portals-desktop.html.
Visible text sometimes differs in order from the outerHTML source, suggesting text extraction is based on rendered or normalized content rather than raw DOM order.
standouts: The ledger encodes a design-to-code mapping through ancestor_dossier entries that describe what a region is, what you can do there, and how to change it.
Self-registered candidates carry stable ui:// addresses, turning mockup elements into addressable design symbols.
The selection rules are explicit and class-aware, distinguishing decorative classes, fragment classes, semantic tags, and control ancestors.
It intentionally preserves duplicates across mockups so a downstream reduction step can decide how to collapse them.
conventions: JSON schema with generated_from, count, filter, and candidates array.
ui:// URI scheme for ancestor_address, base_address, and self_address.
ancestor_dossier howto fields follow a WHAT / WHAT YOU CAN DO / HOW TO CHANGE IT structure.
Semantic HTML tags are treated as primary carriers of meaning; div and span are admitted only under a direct-text threshold.
Class names are categorized as decorative, fragment, or meaningful to guide inclusion/exclusion.
Mockup file names use an A-prefixed identifier pattern.
concerns: The 12-character direct-text threshold for div/span inclusion is arbitrary and may not generalize across languages or compact labels.
outerHTML values are truncated in the file, so full element content is not recoverable here.
Some candidates have an ancestor_address that does not match their dossier region, such as the footer element using ui://activity while its dossier says canvas.
Excluding elements under button/a/label/summary ancestors may omit meaningful nested labels or hints.
No deduplication means downstream consumers must handle a large, possibly redundant candidate set.
notes: A design-to-code bridge ledger: every mockup element is annotated with the region it belongs to, what that region means, and where to change it.
questions: What is RG5 REDUCE and how does it collapse duplicate surfaces?
Is visible_text derived from rendered text, textContent, or another extraction method?
Why does the footer candidate use ancestor_address ui://activity while its ancestor_dossier names the canvas region?
Does the 12-character direct-text threshold hold for all mockups and locales?
Are void_tags listed only for documentation, or do they actively affect candidate extraction?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to add an interpretive layer for a ledger of code/design substrates; the file does exactly that by recording extraction rules and annotated UI candidates, so purpose matches actual.
apparent_themes: Semantic HTML as an API surface
intention_signals: It tries to make every visible UI element accountable to a code region and capability, so design mockups can be queried, navigated, and eventually fused with implementation.
novelty: The combination of HTML-mockup element extraction with a structured dossier that narrates each region's purpose, affordances, and change path, plus a self-registered ui:// address scheme for design elements.
contribution: It contributes a structured, addressable inventory of design mockup elements and their semantic and implementation context, which a fused system could use to correlate visual surfaces with code regions and capabilities while preserving recurring instances for later reduction.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON ledger of 940 UI candidates extracted from 23 design mockups, annotated with selection rules, ancestor region dossiers, and self-registered ui:// addresses.
intention_for_embedding: To bridge design mockups and implementation by giving every extracted UI element a stable address, region context, and change guidance.
